description:
Advances: specled.append_only

Deliverable:
Ship the pure AppendOnly module with all 10 detectors, the fixture builder used by its test suite, and the `normalize_for_state/1` refactor that gives AppendOnly (and tests) a canonical shape for `state.json` payloads.

Concrete additions:
- new module `SpecLedEx.AppendOnly` with public `analyze(prior_state, current_state, decisions, opts) :: [finding]` and 10 internal detectors (requirement_deleted, must_downgraded, scenario_regression, negative_removed, disabled_without_reason, no_baseline, adr_affects_widened, same_pr_self_authorization, missing_change_type, decision_deleted).
- extract `SpecLedEx.normalize_for_state/1` from existing code in lib/specled_ex.ex or lib/specled_ex/state.ex; pure function, contract-tested, no behavior change to current callers.
- new `test/support/append_only_fixtures.ex` with `state_fixture/1`, `requirement/2`, `scenario/1`, `adr/1` per T4.
- new `test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs` with ~24 per-detector x authorization-outcome cases through the fixture builder.
- update `.spec/specs/append_only.spec.md` spec-verification stubs: flip execute: false -> execute: true for the test-file targets.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs test/specled_ex_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate (from append_only.spec.md):
  - requirement_deleted (unauthorized vs. authorized paths)
  - must_downgraded (must -> should, must -> none, must -> must_not)
  - scenario_regression (count drop triggers)
  - negative_removed (polarity: negative disappears)
  - disabled_without_reason (execute: false without reason:)
  - no_baseline (prior :missing -> exactly one :info, no other findings)
  - adr_affects_widened (affects change on accepted ADR)
  - same_pr_self_authorization (ADR new in diff self-authorizing a removal)
  - missing_change_type (ADR referenced by authorization lookup without change_type)
  - decision_deleted (ADR id absent from current state)
  - identity property (analyze(s, s, []) == [])
  - findings_sorted (output sorted by {subject_id, entity_id, code})
  - fix_block_discipline (every message ends with code-fenced fix: block)
- Merge gate: T1 round-trip invariant - normalize_for_state(index) == Jason.decode!(File.read!(write_state(...))) over a fixture corpus. Add as a test in test/specled_ex_test.exs.
- Property tests: identity (T8 #2), bootstrap (T8 #3), CrossField idempotence (T8 #4) - idempotence may live in W1's CrossField test file.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex (W2b)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (W2b)
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex (W3)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (W3)
- .spec/specs/overlap.spec.md (W2b flips its stubs)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex.ex (normalize_for_state/1 extraction, no behavior change to other callers)
- test/support/append_only_fixtures.ex (new)
- test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex_test.exs (round-trip invariant gate)
- .spec/specs/append_only.spec.md (flip verification execute: true)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not wire AppendOnly into BranchCheck.run/3 yet (W2b owns that wiring).
- Do not add the `Spec-Drift:` trailer override integration test (W2b).
- Do not touch severity defaults or config-driven severity overrides (W3).
- Do not introduce git I/O into AppendOnly; the module is pure over two state payloads plus a decisions list.
- Do not add any finding code not in the 12 ratified by specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.
- Do not cache modal_class on requirements or in state.json (see specled.decision.modal_class_diff_time).

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] T1 round-trip invariant test green
- [ ] R1.f message-format test green (every append_only/* finding ends with fix: block)
- [ ] findings-sort contract test green
- [ ] Spec verification passes for specled.append_only subject
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Must land together with W2b on the feature branch before merging to main (user directive 2026-04-23). Do not open a PR to main from W2a alone.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: fbeda291-0885-45f1-9dec-8701e449cd89
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/fbeda291-0885-45f1-9dec-8701e449cd89.jsonl

Commit: 88df64af7162393550f4c5732c254d3173595ef9

Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex (new) — 10 detectors + helpers; sorted, total, fix-block discipline
- lib/specled_ex.ex — normalize_for_state/1 extracted as public; normalize_decisions now carries change_type/reverses_what/replaces (additive)
- test_support/append_only_fixtures.ex (new) — state_fixture/1, requirement/3, scenario/1, adr/1 (form: :state | :parsed)
- test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs (new) — 32 unit tests + 2 StreamData properties
- test/specled_ex_test.exs — 3 T1 round-trip invariant tests (empty corpus, with scenarios/verifications, with ADRs carrying change_type)
- test/test_helper.exs — added Code.require_file for the new fixture
- .spec/specs/append_only.spec.md — flipped execute: false → true on both verification stubs; surface path matches project convention
- .spec/state.json — regenerated by `mix spec.validate`

Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.AppendOnlyTest (32 tests + 2 properties covering every detector × authorized/unauthorized pair, identity, findings sort, fix-block discipline)
- SpecLedExTest "normalize_for_state/1 round-trip" (3 tests)

Verification stubs flipped:
- .spec/specs/append_only.spec.md: both `spec-verification` stubs (command target + source_file target) set to execute: true

Merge-gate results:
- Compile: clean (`mix compile --warnings-as-errors` silent)
- Tests: 480/480 green (`mix test`), 2/2 properties green
- mix spec.check: specled.append_only subject is clean (no findings). 3 pre-existing warnings remain against specled.overlap (surface + verification target missing for lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex and test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs) — those files are explicitly Out of Scope here and are owned by W2b (specled_-fm4.4). Pre-existed at fm4.2 HEAD; no regression introduced.
- T1 round-trip invariant: green (test_specled_ex_test.exs)
- Fix-block discipline: green (every append_only/* message ends in ```…``` with a fix: line; tested both per-finding and across a broad fixture)
- Findings-sort contract: green (sort-stable property asserted over multi-subject diff)

Summary:
Built SpecLedEx.AppendOnly as a pure diff-time function over two normalized state payloads plus a head-side decisions list. Extracted SpecLedEx.normalize_for_state/1 from write_state so AppendOnly and tests can consume the canonical state shape without writing to disk; added change_type/reverses_what/replaces to decision items in state.json so accepted-ADR structural drift is detectable (additive, non-breaking). All 10 ratified append_only/* finding codes emit per spec, authorization lookups respect the 4-value weakening set, same-PR self-authorization surfaces as a warning (no requirement_deleted for those ids), and missing change_type on consulted ADRs warns without authorizing. No Git I/O, no caching of modal_class, no BranchCheck wiring (that’s W2b).

Minor scope deviations (flagged for auditor):
- Fixture path: ticket listed test/support/append_only_fixtures.ex but the project convention is test_support/ (sibling to test/, loaded by test_helper.exs via Code.require_file). I used test_support/ and updated the spec's `surface:` entry to match. The mechanical scope would flag this file as outside "Allowed touches"; it is the test/support analogue, placed at the directory this repo has always used.
- test/test_helper.exs is not in "Allowed touches" but needed one `Code.require_file("../test_support/append_only_fixtures.ex", __DIR__)` line so the new fixture compiles before the test suite runs. Trivial plumbing, matches the existing pattern for specled_ex_case.ex.

VERIFY: PASS

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 8
- Files outside Allowed globs: 2 (minor, both defensible)
  - test_support/append_only_fixtures.ex — ticket listed test/support/append_only_fixtures.ex, but the project convention is test_support/ (sibling of test/, pre-existing specled_ex_case.ex and specled_ex_integration_case.ex live there; test/support/ does not exist). Legitimate path-convention alignment.
  - test/test_helper.exs — one-line Code.require_file for the new fixture, matches the existing pattern for specled_ex_case.ex. Trivial plumbing, no behavior change.
  Also note: .spec/state.json is regenerated as the documented side-effect of `mix spec.validate` (the change is purely the additive change_type/reverses_what/replaces fields flowing through normalize_decisions, which is within scope of the normalize_for_state refactor).
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no touch to lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex, lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex, lib/specled_ex/config.ex, lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, .spec/specs/overlap.spec.md)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2, passed: 2 (mix test test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs → 32 tests + 2 properties, 0 failures; mix test test/specled_ex_test.exs → 4 tests, 0 failures)
- Requirements with passing tests: 13/13
  - specled.append_only.requirement_deleted → test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs:11 (unauthorized) + :28 (authorized suppresses) + :60 (clarifies does not authorize)
  - specled.append_only.must_downgraded → :81 (MUST→SHOULD), :101 (MUST→none), :120 (MUST→MUST NOT cross-polarity), :139 (ADR suppression)
  - specled.append_only.scenario_regression → :166 (count drop), :193 (stable clean), :206 (increase clean), :229 (ADR suppression)
  - specled.append_only.negative_removed → :260 (explicit), :281 (inferred MUST NOT), :300 (ADR suppression)
  - specled.append_only.disabled_without_reason → :329 (warn), :349 (reason OK), :366 (execute:true clean)
  - specled.append_only.no_baseline → :378 (exactly one info), :391 (variant tag first_run/shallow_clone/bad_ref), :404 (suppresses all other findings), plus property :736
  - specled.append_only.adr_affects_widened → :421 (affects change), :438 (change_type change), :454 (non-accepted can drift)
  - specled.append_only.same_pr_self_authorization → :472 (emit + suppress requirement_deleted), :503 (affects-exact-match required)
  - specled.append_only.missing_change_type → :533 (emits + does not authorize), :559 (not consulted = no warning)
  - specled.append_only.decision_deleted → :579 (prior absent from current), :593 (present = clean)
  - specled.append_only.identity → :603 (populated state identity) + property :728
  - specled.append_only.findings_sorted → :624 (sort contract over multi-subject diff)
  - specled.append_only.fix_block_discipline → :678 (broad-mix assertion over all findings) + per-test fix_block_present? assertions on every positive finding
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - grep "AppendOnly" in lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex → no matches (not wired yet, W2b owns that)
  - Spec-Drift: trailer exists in pre-existing branch_check/trailer.ex; no new integration test introduced and no trailer-AppendOnly wiring
  - No diff against lib/specled_ex/config.ex or lib/specled_ex/config/** (severities untouched)
  - No System.cmd / Porcelain / File.read / File.stream! / File.open / File.write in lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex
  - Exactly 10 append_only/* codes in the module (requirement_deleted, must_downgraded, scenario_regression, negative_removed, disabled_without_reason, no_baseline, adr_affects_widened, same_pr_self_authorization, missing_change_type, decision_deleted) — matches the ratified budget
  - No modal_class field or cache added to lib/specled_ex.ex or the normalized state structure; ModalClass.classify/1 runs afresh at diff time in append_only.ex
- Project verification: PASS-with-pre-existing-warnings.
  - mix spec.check at HEAD (88df64a): 3 warnings, all against specled.overlap surface/verification targets (lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex, test/specled_ex/overlap_test.exs — W2b territory, explicitly Out of Scope here).
  - mix spec.check at parent (29a3916, via `git worktree add`): 7 warnings — the same 3 overlap warnings PLUS 4 pre-existing append_only warnings (surface/verification targets missing). This ticket strictly reduces the warning set from 7 to 3 and introduces no new warnings. Confirmed not a regression.

T1 round-trip invariant gate: GREEN. test/specled_ex_test.exs :34-157 asserts `SpecLedEx.normalize_for_state(index) == Jason.decode!(File.read!(write_state(index, nil, ...)))` over three corpora: empty, subject+requirement+scenario+verification, and structured ADR fields (change_type survives the round-trip).

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The two "outside Allowed" files (test_support/append_only_fixtures.ex, test/test_helper.exs) are both defensible and the implementer called them out honestly in the IMPLEMENTED comment. The path-convention alignment is correct — test_support/ is the pre-existing project convention.
- `.spec/state.json` changes are the expected generated output from normalize_decisions now carrying change_type/reverses_what/replaces; that is in-scope for the normalize_for_state extraction.
- All 10 ratified finding codes emit; fix-block discipline is enforced by both per-test assertions and a broad-mix test at :678; findings sort contract is enforced by the sort test at :624; identity is both unit-tested (:603) and property-tested (:728); bootstrap is both unit-tested (:404) and property-tested (:736).

title: W2a: AppendOnly module + fixture builder + normalize_for_state
